Server listening on port 8080

After 500 requests: listeners=501, heap=6.07MB
FAIL: Listener count grew to 501 (expected <= 10)
      Event listeners are being registered per-request and never removed

#!/bin/sh
# Test for memory leak via event listener accumulation
# Sends many requests and checks that listener count and memory stay bounded.

set -e

FAIL=0
PORT=8080

# Start server in background
node /app/app/server.js &
SERVER_PID=$!
sleep 1

# Verify server is running
if ! kill -0 $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "FAIL: Server failed to start"
    exit 1
fi

# Get baseline stats
BASELINE=$(curl -s http://localhost:$PORT/stats)
BASELINE_LISTENERS=$(echo "$BASELINE" | jq '.listenerCoun

total 20
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root   94 Mar  9 02:37 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root   14 Mar 13 04:54 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1156 Mar  9 02:37 monitor.js
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 2273 Mar  9 02:37 processor.js
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  902 Mar  9 02:37 queue.js
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 2234 Mar  9 02:36 server.js
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  707 Mar  9 02:37 utils.js

Server listening on port 8080
Baseline: listeners=2, heap=4.04MB
After 500 requests: listeners=2, heap=5.25MB
PASS: Listener count stable at 2
PASS: No event listener leak detected
